title: Steal This Episode

jokes.text: The only reason to have done stuff over the weekend.
jokes.setup: Describing Monday morning water cooler chit-chat
jokes.punchline: Reducing all weekend activities to mere conversation fuel
jokes.explanation: Clever observational humor about the performative nature of weekend activities, delivered with economy

jokes.text: Actually, this one undoes the stuff from the last one, so it's a de-boot.
jokes.punchline: Making up 'de-boot' as the opposite of reboot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy pedantry creating a clever portmanteau that satirizes Hollywood's reboot obsession

jokes.text: But what if I want some cooled water? / You should have thought of that when you were not seeing the movie.
jokes.setup: Being banished from the water cooler for not seeing a movie
jokes.punchline: Treating movie-watching as prerequisite for water access
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of spoiler culture taken to logical extreme with water cooler exclusion

jokes.text: No, no, don't stop! I'm sorry I got mad. I'll do that thing you like.
jokes.setup: Homer desperately trying to hear movie spoilers
jokes.punchline: Offering sexual favors to continue overhearing spoilers
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer desperation moment with uncomfortable sexual undertones

jokes.text: ♪ Amazing grace / How loud I sing / To drown the spoilers out! ♪
jokes.setup: Ned trying to avoid hearing movie spoilers
jokes.punchline: Replacing hymn lyrics with spoiler-avoidance strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific solution combining Ned's religiosity with relatable spoiler anxiety in perfect lyrical form

jokes.text: Emily-Beth? We can't afford Emily-Beth! She brings her own puppets!
jokes.punchline: Puppet-bringing as the ultimate luxury babysitting service
jokes.explanation: Unexpected detail that makes expensive childcare absurdly specific and relatable

jokes.text: Two adults, two kids, and one senior with the mind of a baby so he should be free.
jokes.punchline: Arguing Grampa should get child pricing due to mental capacity
jokes.explanation: Homer's cheapskate logic applied to family dynamics with brutal honesty about Grampa

jokes.text: For the 3-D show, that'll be $72. / What?! / Or, for ten dollars more, you can see it in IMAX Hobbit Frame Rate Virtual Reality Vision.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd movie format names with minimal price difference
jokes.explanation: Satirizes movie theater pricing schemes with invented format mashups

jokes.text: The motorcycle dudes are chicks! I wonder what else is chicks?
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent curiosity about widespread gender swapping
jokes.explanation: Child-like wonder at Hollywood gender-swapping trends

jokes.text: Also available in 'Arctic Slut,' 'Morning After Melon' and 'Elon Musk.'
jokes.setup: Body spray commercial with scent names
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate/bizarre scent names ending with tech billionaire
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity in product naming with unexpected celebrity reference

jokes.text: If I wanted to pay for commercials I can't skip, I'd sign up for Hulu Plus!
jokes.setup: Complaining about unskippable movie theater ads
jokes.punchline: Comparing to streaming service's ad model
jokes.explanation: Timely observation about evolving media consumption models

jokes.text: This guy's on his cell phone, she's texting, he's sexting, and that guy brought a baby to a 9:00 movie!
jokes.punchline: Escalating from phone use to sexting to inappropriate baby timing
jokes.explanation: Relatable catalog of movie theater annoyances with good escalation

jokes.text: The FOX network forbids the broadcast of step-by-step instructions for illegally downloading copyrighted intellectual property. In the meantime, please enjoy this footage from NASCAR's 2011 Martinsville Cup.
jokes.punchline: Network censoring its own content with random NASCAR footage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on network self-censorship with absurdly specific replacement content

jokes.text: That was the greatest thing I've seen on a computer that I can talk about with you in the room.
jokes.setup: Homer praising the pirated movie download
jokes.punchline: Implying worse computer activities he can't mention to Bart
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment balancing innocent praise with adult implications

jokes.text: How did you know how to do that? / I'm under 30.
jokes.punchline: Age as only qualification needed for tech savvy
jokes.explanation: Efficient generational humor with perfect economy

jokes.text: I like that James Bond is ugly now. I like that he doesn't have any gadgets. I like that he's not good at shooting, doesn't say funny quips, or do any James Bond stuff.
jokes.punchline: Listing everything that makes Bond not Bond-like as positives
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of gritty reboots through Comic Book Guy's contrarian perspective

jokes.text: All I need to see this movie is a laptop and a Web site based in a country that's really just an offshore oil platform.
jokes.punchline: Describing piracy sites' dubious legal jurisdictions
jokes.explanation: Absurdist take on piracy site legal loopholes with specific imagery

jokes.text: It mixes the wonder of movie-going with the rush of stealing.
jokes.setup: Praising the home theater piracy experience
jokes.punchline: Openly celebrating the criminal aspect
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about finding joy in criminal activity, perfectly delivered

jokes.text: No more jerks talking on their cell phones! / No more jerks telling me not to talk on my cell phone!
jokes.punchline: Second character revealing he's the annoying cell phone user
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff revealing the perspective flip on theater etiquette

jokes.text: Tonight's movie is Life is Funny, directed by Judd Apatow. It's based on his life, starring his family and ad-libbed by his friends. So for the next three and a half hours, enjoy!
jokes.setup: Introducing a movie at the backyard theater
jokes.punchline: Satirizing Apatow's filmmaking style and notorious length
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of Apatow's autobiographical tendencies and film length

jokes.text: Did you just pass gas while we were making love? / You're the one who hired the Korean taco truck for our private school fund-raiser. / I live it, he writes it.
jokes.punchline: Apatow breaking fourth wall to explain it's from real life
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on autobiographical filmmaking with cringe elements

jokes.text: We may be going to Hell for seeing 'side bosom,' but this backyard is a little slice of Heaven, all thanks to Homer Simpson!
jokes.punchline: Religious guilt about mild movie content vs. actual crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment showing misplaced moral priorities

jokes.text: When I watched that pirated movie, I was stealing with my eyes.
jokes.punchline: Treating viewing as physical theft act
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Marge guilt-logic turning viewing into physical crime

jokes.text: Now this money will end up in the hands of the talented people who make magic happen on the silver screen.
jokes.setup: Marge paying for the pirated movie she watched
jokes.punchline: Money going to street performer instead of Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Visual irony showing how misguided guilt payments actually work

jokes.text: I wanted to catch serial killers. / 'I wanted to catch serial killers.'
jokes.setup: Agent explaining motivation for FBI work
jokes.punchline: Boss mocking noble law enforcement goals
jokes.explanation: Satirizes misplaced law enforcement priorities with good character dynamic

jokes.text: From now on, you will lay down your lives to protect America's chick flicks, buddy comedies and steady stream of animated misfires.
jokes.setup: FBI boss giving dramatic mission speech
jokes.punchline: Heroic rhetoric for protecting mediocre Hollywood content
jokes.explanation: Effective contrast between heroic tone and mundane/bad movies being protected

jokes.text: My childhood has been un-ruined!
jokes.setup: Watching better version of beloved franchise
jokes.punchline: Creating 'un-ruined' as opposite of franchise destruction
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay about franchise rehabilitation

jokes.text: I'm not wearing an undershirt and my badge is poking into my skin.
jokes.setup: Agent trying to be intimidating during arrest
jokes.punchline: Undermining authority with personal wardrobe malfunction
jokes.explanation: Humanizing moment that undercuts law enforcement authority

jokes.text: Superintendent Chalmers, you never forgave me for that time we wore the same Hawaiian shirt to that luau!
jokes.setup: Homer accusing someone of turning him in
jokes.punchline: Blaming betrayal on embarrassing fashion incident
jokes.explanation: Absurd motivation for betrayal with specific embarrassing details

jokes.text: Copyrighted material! Don't look at it! Someone sell me a ticket! Ear plugs in! Blinders on! We trained for this!
jokes.punchline: Treating copyrighted content like hazardous material
jokes.explanation: Brilliant physical comedy treating intellectual property like toxic waste

jokes.text: My brother lost his job as a grip on a movie set because of piracy. He had to sell his Jet Ski. A grip without a Jet Ski ain't no grip at all!
jokes.setup: Explaining piracy's impact on film industry workers
jokes.punchline: Essential nature of Jet Ski ownership for grips
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation about industry worker stereotypes with perfect specificity

jokes.text: What are you guys in for? Bank robbery. Drug trafficking. What about you? Movie piracy. What?! People in show business work hard to make those movies.
jokes.punchline: Criminals being outraged by movie piracy
jokes.explanation: Great ironic reversal - violent criminals having moral standards about intellectual property

jokes.text: Only with less growling when I go near his food.
jokes.setup: Comparing Homer's absence to having him there
jokes.punchline: Treating Homer like an aggressive pet around food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization through family member's perspective

jokes.text: It's not even the worst kind of pirate Dad's ever been.
jokes.punchline: Reference to Homer being other types of pirates
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to show history with efficient delivery

jokes.text: There's no shampoo, just soap!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he can't return to jail
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing hair care products over freedom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer vanity priorities in crisis situation

jokes.text: You're as safe here as you would be in the Skatteskrapan.
jokes.punchline: Using untranslatable Swedish word for reference point
jokes.explanation: Mild cultural humor but doesn't translate well for most viewers

jokes.text: Bah, your Hollywood studios are the real thieves, claiming all their hit movies have lost money. I spit on their bookkeeping.
jokes.punchline: Calling out Hollywood accounting practices
jokes.explanation: Smart insider reference to Hollywood's creative accounting

jokes.text: Why don't they make a sequel to Taken where The Hangover guys get taken and the only ones that could rescue them is Fast and Furious.
jokes.punchline: Absurd franchise mashup with role reversal
jokes.explanation: Creative franchise mashup idea that sounds plausible for Hollywood

jokes.text: And why don't they make a sequel to Taken where The Hangover guys get taken and the only ones that could rescue them is Fast and Furious.
jokes.punchline: Absurd mash-up of popular franchises
jokes.explanation: Fun franchise mash-up idea, captures Hollywood's sequel obsession

jokes.text: ♪ Respecting the law, respecting the law / Copyright law, copyright law ♪
jokes.setup: Death metal being used to drive out fugitives
jokes.punchline: Death metal about following copyright law
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of rebellious music genre with law-abiding message

jokes.text: Oh-oh, Swedes love death metal. It reminds us of death.
jokes.setup: Death metal failing to drive out Swedes
jokes.punchline: Deadpan explanation of Swedish death metal appreciation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with cultural stereotype subversion

jokes.text: Damn those Peace Prize-giving fish-smokers.
jokes.punchline: Angry but accurate description of Swedish culture
jokes.explanation: Amusing cultural generalization delivered with frustration

jokes.text: Consulates are regional offices, which serve the embassy in the capitol.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's automatic correction in crisis situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment prioritizing accuracy over situation

jokes.text: It was me! I was the one who told the FBI.
jokes.punchline: Marge's guilt-driven confession
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment with good dramatic timing

jokes.text: Who would've thought the authorities would use a confession against me?
jokes.punchline: Naive surprise about how law enforcement works
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge naivety about criminal justice system

jokes.text: To love and cherish? To aid and abet?
jokes.punchline: Including criminal assistance in marriage vows
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mixing wedding vows with criminal terminology

jokes.text: Well, you did! And they got laughs. Solid laughs!
jokes.setup: Marge saying she didn't want funny vows
jokes.punchline: Homer's pride in the vows' comedic success
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's priorities even in emotional moments

jokes.text: I never wanted to say funny vows. / Well, you did! And they got laughs. Solid laughs!
jokes.punchline: Homer defending the quality of the comedy
jokes.explanation: Homer's pride in comedy quality even in emotional moment

jokes.text: Art created by writers, directors, and the guy who uses a computer to erase or enhance nipples.
jokes.punchline: Including digital nipple editor as artist
jokes.explanation: Unexpected inclusion of behind-the-scenes digital work in artistic pantheon

jokes.text: The personal rabbis, Seth Rogen.
jokes.setup: Listing people who depend on Hollywood
jokes.punchline: Seth Rogen as standalone dependent category
jokes.explanation: Celebrity name-drop with implication of special dependency status

jokes.text: They left off my director's commentary! It didn't even have a blooper reel!
jokes.punchline: Missing bonus features as primary concern
jokes.explanation: Actor's vanity about bonus content over main theft

jokes.text: An Erin Brockovich but with more cleavage!
jokes.punchline: Reducing legal drama to sexualization
jokes.explanation: Hollywood executive crassness about film marketing

jokes.text: Good Lord, he's hitting all four quadrants!
jokes.punchline: Marketing terminology applied to personal tragedy
jokes.explanation: Industry jargon satirizing how Hollywood views human stories

jokes.text: Will Smith's family wants to play your family. / I'd be Jaden.
jokes.punchline: Jaden Smith's eager self-insertion
jokes.explanation: Celebrity cameo humor but fairly predictable

jokes.text: I'll sign with whoever can convince Channing Tatum to gain the most weight to play me.
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing actor's weight gain over other considerations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer vanity moment about physical representation

jokes.text: Yeah, we ripped a version off Bootleg Bay just like you taught us.
jokes.setup: Showing Homer's movie at surprise screening
jokes.punchline: Using Homer's piracy lessons against his own movie
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic callback showing consequences of Homer's teaching

jokes.text: You're pirating my movie? The hell you are! Piracy is stealing! You're taking money out of my family's mouths!
jokes.setup: Homer discovering his movie being pirated
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal of his earlier piracy stance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character hypocrisy moment with complete philosophical reversal

jokes.text: prof-it par-tic-i-pa-tion.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his new concern about piracy
jokes.punchline: Carefully pronouncing complex financial term
jokes.explanation: Homer's careful pronunciation of financial jargon showing new priorities

jokes.text: No bargain matinees! And tell your friends it was great! Buy the stuff they advertise in the commercials before it! Homer in the movie wears Ask Body Spray and so should you!
jokes.setup: Homer demanding people see his movie properly
jokes.punchline: Increasingly demanding marketing requirements
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's complete transformation to industry shill

jokes.text: What have we learned about not blindly supporting our husband?
jokes.setup: Marge starting to question Homer's demands
jokes.punchline: Homer turning marriage lesson against independent thinking
jokes.explanation: Homer weaponizing earlier marriage conflict for commercial purposes

jokes.text: Well, both groups claim their intentions are noble, but at the end of the day, they're both trying to steal as much money as they can.
jokes.setup: Asked about who are the good guys in piracy debate
jokes.punchline: Cynical assessment that everyone's motivated by greed
jokes.explanation: Mature Lisa wisdom cutting through moral posturing to economic reality

jokes.text: So... everyone's a pirate?
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining universal greed in piracy debate
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex moral issue to simple pirate metaphor
jokes.explanation: Neat thematic conclusion tying episode title to moral ambiguity

tmdb_overview: Homer becomes annoyed with movie theaters, so Bart teaches him how to download movies illegally. Homer is delighted with his discovery of free movies until he's caught for piracy. Deputy Director Gratman, the FBI's anti-piracy boss, is tasked with hunting down Homer, who stands accused of illegally downloading a film.
